handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `rol_`
creating → refuse when name fails valid_role_name rule
creating → refuse when the (application_id, tenant_id, name, guard_name) composite already exists (defense against the DB unique constraint race)
creating → consume 1 unit of custom_role_slot entitlement when is_system=false + fire CustomRoleQuotaExceeded on cap
creating → refuse when tenant lacks custom_roles entitlement + is_system=false
creating → fire RoleCreated + TenantCustomRoleCreated (when is_system=false)
updating (name / guard_name / application_id changed) → refuse when is_system=true — fire SystemRoleModificationRefused
updating (any change) → fire RoleUpdated
deleting → refuse when is_system=true — fire SystemRoleModificationRefused
deleting → refuse when any active model_has_roles rows reference this role (`ROLE_IN_USE`)
deleting (custom role, no active assignments) → release 1 unit of custom_role_slot entitlement + fire RoleDeleted + TenantCustomRoleDeleted
restoring → refuse when name conflicts with a live row
any mutation → fire PermissionCacheInvalidated for every principal holding the role
creating → populate ULID prefix `per_`
creating → refuse when name fails valid_permission_name rule (dot notation + valid scope suffix + no reserved prefix)
creating → refuse when the (application_id, tenant_id, name, guard_name) composite already exists
creating → refuse when tenant lacks custom_permissions entitlement + is_system=false (only Enterprise can create new permission strings)
creating (is_system=false) → fire PermissionCreated
creating (is_system=true) → fire PermissionCreated + emit `stackra.rbac.permissions.total` gauge update
updating (name / guard_name / application_id changed) → refuse when is_system=true — fire SystemRoleModificationRefused (same event; the permission surface uses the same alert path)
deleting → refuse when is_system=true — fire SystemPermissionDeletionRefused error
deleting → refuse when any role_has_permissions or model_has_permissions rows reference this permission (`PERMISSION_IN_USE`)
any mutation → fire PermissionCacheInvalidated (bulk, all principals — permissions are structurally impactful)
creating → refuse when role.guard_name !== permission.guard_name — fire GuardMismatchRefused
creating → refuse when role.application_id !== permission.application_id (both non-null) — fire ApplicationMismatchRefused

creating → refuse when role.tenant_id !== permission.tenant_id AND both non-null (permissions can be system-scoped tenant_id NULL and roles can be tenant-scoped, but a tenant-scoped role can never bind a different tenant's permission)
creating → refuse when role.is_system=true AND permission.is_system=false AND tenant lacks custom_permissions entitlement
creating → refuse when role's current permission count >= config('rbac.roles.max_permissions_per_role') — `ROLE_TOO_MANY_PERMISSIONS`
creating → denormalise application_id + tenant_id from the role for query-plan efficiency
created → fire PermissionsAttachedToRole (batched — the service dispatches ONE event per attach transaction covering all permissions)
deleted → fire PermissionsDetachedFromRole (same batching)
any mutation → fire PermissionCacheInvalidated for every principal holding the role
creating → refuse when role.guard_name doesn't match the principal's guard (`GuardMismatch`)
creating → refuse when role.application_id doesn't match the principal's application_id (for User principals) — `PRINCIPAL_APPLICATION_MISMATCH`
creating → refuse when the role_assignable_by_actor rule fails (no privilege escalation)
creating → refuse when principal's active role count >= config('rbac.roles.max_active_per_principal') — `PRINCIPAL_TOO_MANY_ROLES`
creating → refuse when expires_at is set AND tenant lacks time_bounded_role_assignments entitlement
creating → refuse when expires_at > now() + config('rbac.assignments.max_time_bounded_duration_days')
creating → denormalise application_id + tenant_id from the role
creating → populate assigned_by + assigned_at from the current actor (auth()->user()) if not explicitly set
created → fire RoleAssignedToPrincipal
updated (expires_at changed) → emit `stackra.rbac.assignments.expiration_extended` metric
deleting → fire RoleRevokedFromPrincipal with the resolved trigger
any mutation → fire PermissionCacheInvalidated for the affected principal
creating → refuse on guard mismatch + application mismatch (same rules as ModelHasRolesObserver)
creating → denormalise application_id + tenant_id
created → fire PermissionGrantedToPrincipal
deleted → fire PermissionRevokedFromPrincipal
any mutation → fire PermissionCacheInvalidated for the affected principal
creating → populate ULID prefix `rdf_`
creating → verify role_id references a valid, is_system=true role
creating → verify every entry in default_permissions[] exists in the permissions table (config('rbac.seeding.mode') = strict enforces this; permissive logs a warning and drops missing entries)
creating → verify every entry in business_type_slugs[] exists in tenancy's business_type catalogue
creating → verify min_tier is one of ['small', 'medium', 'business', 'enterprise']
creating → invalidate rbac:role_definitions:{application_id} cache
updating (default_permissions or business_type_slugs changed) → invalidate rbac:role_definitions:{application_id} + optionally re-run TenantDefaultRoleSeeder for affected tenants (config-gated)
deleting → refuse when any active role_definitions references this row (must archive first)
any mutation → emit `stackra.rbac.role_definitions.updated` metric
